questions.question: If |A| = 4, what is |𝒫(A)| (the size of the powerset)?
questions.explanation: |𝒫(A)| = 2^|A|. Each subset is determined by an independent binary choice 'is this element in?' for each of the |A| elements. 2^4 = 16. This exponential blow-up is why 'find any subset satisfying a constraint' is the standard NP-hard problem template.

questions.question: Which of these is a function from A = {1, 2, 3} to B = {a, b, c}?
questions.explanation: A function from A to B requires every input in A to map to exactly one output. (1) is missing input 3 (not total). (2) has 1 mapping to both a and b (not single-valued). (4) is a function from B to A, not A to B. Only (3) is correct.

questions.question: Let f: ℤ → ℤ, f(x) = 2x. Which property does f have?
questions.explanation: Injective: f(a) = f(b) ⇒ 2a = 2b ⇒ a = b ✓. Surjective: NO — odd integers aren't in the image (no integer x has 2x = 3). So f is injective but not surjective.

questions.question: Relational composition of R ⊆ A × B and S ⊆ B × C gives `S ∘ R = {(a, c) : ∃b. (a, b) ∈ R ∧ (b, c) ∈ S}`. Which SQL operation is this?
options: JOIN ... ON R.b = S.b (projected onto a and c)
questions.explanation: Relational composition is exactly the (equi-)JOIN of two binary relations on their shared column. SQL inherited this from Codd's relational algebra, which is set theory applied to tables.

questions.question: A function f has a two-sided inverse (f⁻¹ with f⁻¹ ∘ f = id and f ∘ f⁻¹ = id) iff f is what?
questions.explanation: Two-sided inverses exist iff f is bijective. A left inverse (g ∘ f = id) exists iff f is injective; a right inverse (f ∘ g = id) iff f is surjective (modulo choice for infinite sets). Having both = bijection.
